reason: The item cleanly tests boundary-aware causal attribution: whether lower mass loss can be exclusively reduced to stored energy versus requiring an additional ignition/ejection mechanism. Decoys are mostly single-fault and engineering-plausible (exclusive-cause claim, false prerequisite, and unjustified universal temperature inference), producing good expert discrimination, though two wrong options lean on broadly similar overreach patterns which slightly reduces separability at the top end.

The item cleanly targets boundary-aware engineering judgment: a measurement method’s physical precondition (ignition visibility) and how that blocks downstream mass-flow derivation, plus what temperature-only evidence can and cannot justify about hazard. It has strong discriminative power against common overreaches (analogy-based velocity inference; equating lower temperatures or no ignition with safety/non-flammability), with only minor risk that some respondents argue alternative non-optical tracers could exist without being clearly excluded by the prompt.
